speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Barkloughly Castle call they this at hand?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Yea, my lord. How brooks your Grace the air

speaker: AUMERLE
text: After your late tossing on the breaking seas?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Needs must I like it well. I weep for joy

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To stand upon my kingdom once again.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Dear earth, I do salute thee with my hand,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Though rebels wound thee with their horses’ hoofs.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As a long-parted mother with her child

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Plays fondly with her tears and smiles in meeting,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: So, weeping, smiling, greet I thee, my earth,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And do thee favors with my royal hands.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Feed not thy sovereign’s foe, my gentle earth,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Nor with thy sweets comfort his ravenous sense,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But let thy spiders, that suck up thy venom,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And heavy-gaited toads lie in their way,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Doing annoyance to the treacherous feet

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Which with usurping steps do trample thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Yield stinging nettles to mine enemies,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And when they from thy bosom pluck a flower,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Guard it, I pray thee, with a lurking adder,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Whose double tongue may with a mortal touch

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Throw death upon thy sovereign’s enemies.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Mock not my senseless conjuration, lords.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: This earth shall have a feeling, and these stones

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Prove armèd soldiers, ere her native king

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shall falter under foul rebellion’s arms.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Fear not, my lord. That power that made you king

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Hath power to keep you king in spite of all.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: The means that heavens yield must be embraced

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And not neglected. Else heaven would,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And we will not—heaven’s offer we refuse,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: The proffered means of succor and redress.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: He means, my lord, that we are too remiss,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Whilst Bolingbroke, through our security,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Grows strong and great in substance and in power.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Discomfortable cousin, know’st thou not

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That when the searching eye of heaven is hid

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Behind the globe that lights the lower world,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Then thieves and robbers range abroad unseen

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: In murders and in outrage boldly here?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But when from under this terrestrial ball

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: He fires the proud tops of the eastern pines

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And darts his light through every guilty hole,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Then murders, treasons, and detested sins,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The cloak of night being plucked from off their backs,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Stand bare and naked, trembling at themselves.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: So when this thief, this traitor Bolingbroke,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Who all this while hath reveled in the night

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Whilst we were wand’ring with the Antipodes,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shall see us rising in our throne, the east,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: His treasons will sit blushing in his face,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Not able to endure the sight of day,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But self-affrighted, tremble at his sin.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Not all the water in the rough rude sea

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Can wash the balm off from an anointed king.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The breath of worldly men cannot depose

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The deputy elected by the Lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For every man that Bolingbroke hath pressed

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To lift shrewd steel against our golden crown,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: God for His Richard hath in heavenly pay

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: A glorious angel. Then, if angels fight,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Weak men must fall, for heaven still guards the right.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Welcome, my lord. How far off lies your power?

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Nor near nor farther off, my gracious lord,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Than this weak arm. Discomfort guides my tongue

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And bids me speak of nothing but despair.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: One day too late, I fear me, noble lord,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Hath clouded all thy happy days on earth.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: O, call back yesterday, bid time return,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And thou shalt have twelve thousand fighting men.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Today, today, unhappy day too late,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Overthrows thy joys, friends, fortune, and thy state;

speaker: SALISBURY
text: For all the Welshmen, hearing thou wert dead,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Are gone to Bolingbroke, dispersed, and fled.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Comfort, my liege. Why looks your Grace so pale?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But now the blood of twenty thousand men

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Did triumph in my face, and they are fled;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And till so much blood thither come again

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Have I not reason to look pale and dead?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: All souls that will be safe, fly from my side,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For time hath set a blot upon my pride.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Comfort, my liege. Remember who you are.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I had forgot myself. Am I not king?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Awake, thou coward majesty, thou sleepest!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Is not the King’s name twenty thousand names?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Arm, arm, my name! A puny subject strikes

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: At thy great glory. Look not to the ground,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: You favorites of a king. Are we not high?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: High be our thoughts. I know my Uncle York

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Hath power enough to serve our turn.—But who comes here?

speaker: SCROOP
text: More health and happiness betide my liege

speaker: SCROOP
text: Than can my care-tuned tongue deliver him.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Mine ear is open and my heart prepared.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The worst is worldly loss thou canst unfold.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Say, is my kingdom lost? Why, ’twas my care,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And what loss is it to be rid of care?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Strives Bolingbroke to be as great as we?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Greater he shall not be. If he serve God,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We’ll serve Him too and be his fellow so.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Revolt our subjects? That we cannot mend.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: They break their faith to God as well as us.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Cry woe, destruction, ruin, and decay.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The worst is death, and death will have his day.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Glad am I that your Highness is so armed

speaker: SCROOP
text: To bear the tidings of calamity.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Like an unseasonable stormy day

speaker: SCROOP
text: Which makes the silver rivers drown their shores

speaker: SCROOP
text: As if the world were all dissolved to tears,

speaker: SCROOP
text: So high above his limits swells the rage

speaker: SCROOP
text: Of Bolingbroke, covering your fearful land

speaker: SCROOP
text: With hard bright steel and hearts harder than steel.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Whitebeards have armed their thin and hairless scalps

speaker: SCROOP
text: Against thy Majesty; boys with women’s voices

speaker: SCROOP
text: Strive to speak big and clap their female joints

speaker: SCROOP
text: In stiff unwieldy arms against thy crown;

speaker: SCROOP
text: Thy very beadsmen learn to bend their bows

speaker: SCROOP
text: Of double-fatal yew against thy state.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Yea, distaff women manage rusty bills

speaker: SCROOP
text: Against thy seat. Both young and old rebel,

speaker: SCROOP
text: And all goes worse than I have power to tell.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Too well, too well thou tell’st a tale so ill.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Where is the Earl of Wiltshire? Where is Bagot?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What is become of Bushy? Where is Green,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That they have let the dangerous enemy

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Measure our confines with such peaceful steps?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: If we prevail, their heads shall pay for it!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I warrant they have made peace with Bolingbroke.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Peace have they made with him indeed, my lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: O villains, vipers, damned without redemption!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Dogs easily won to fawn on any man!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Snakes in my heart blood warmed, that sting my heart!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Three Judases, each one thrice worse than Judas!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Would they make peace? Terrible hell

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Make war upon their spotted souls for this!

speaker: SCROOP
text: Sweet love, I see, changing his property,

speaker: SCROOP
text: Turns to the sourest and most deadly hate.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Again uncurse their souls. Their peace is made

speaker: SCROOP
text: With heads and not with hands. Those whom you curse

speaker: SCROOP
text: Have felt the worst of death’s destroying wound

speaker: SCROOP
text: And lie full low, graved in the hollow ground.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Is Bushy, Green, and the Earl of Wiltshire dead?

speaker: SCROOP
text: Ay, all of them at Bristow lost their heads.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Where is the Duke my father with his power?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: No matter where. Of comfort no man speak.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Let’s talk of graves, of worms, and epitaphs,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Make dust our paper, and with rainy eyes

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Write sorrow on the bosom of the earth.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Let’s choose executors and talk of wills.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And yet not so, for what can we bequeath

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Save our deposèd bodies to the ground?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Our lands, our lives, and all are Bolingbroke’s,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And nothing can we call our own but death

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And that small model of the barren earth

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Which serves as paste and cover to our bones.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For God’s sake, let us sit upon the ground

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And tell sad stories of the death of kings—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: How some have been deposed, some slain in war,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Some haunted by the ghosts they have deposed,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Some poisoned by their wives, some sleeping killed,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: All murdered. For within the hollow crown

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That rounds the mortal temples of a king

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Keeps Death his court, and there the antic sits,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Scoffing his state and grinning at his pomp,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Allowing him a breath, a little scene,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To monarchize, be feared, and kill with looks,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Infusing him with self and vain conceit,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As if this flesh which walls about our life

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Were brass impregnable; and humored thus,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Comes at the last and with a little pin

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Bores through his castle wall, and farewell, king!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Cover your heads, and mock not flesh and blood

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With solemn reverence. Throw away respect,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Tradition, form, and ceremonious duty,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For you have but mistook me all this while.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I live with bread like you, feel want,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Taste grief, need friends. Subjected thus,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: How can you say to me I am a king?

speaker: CARLISLE
text: My lord, wise men ne’er sit and wail their woes,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: But presently prevent the ways to wail.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: To fear the foe, since fear oppresseth strength,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Gives in your weakness strength unto your foe,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And so your follies fight against yourself.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Fear, and be slain—no worse can come to fight;

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And fight and die is death destroying death,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Where fearing dying pays death servile breath.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: My father hath a power. Inquire of him,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: And learn to make a body of a limb.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Thou chid’st me well.—Proud Bolingbroke, I come

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To change blows with thee for our day of doom.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: This ague fit of fear is overblown.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: An easy task it is to win our own.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Say, Scroop, where lies our uncle with his power?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Speak sweetly, man, although thy looks be sour.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Men judge by the complexion of the sky

speaker: SCROOP
text: The state and inclination of the day;

speaker: SCROOP
text: So may you by my dull and heavy eye.

speaker: SCROOP
text: My tongue hath but a heavier tale to say.

speaker: SCROOP
text: I play the torturer by small and small

speaker: SCROOP
text: To lengthen out the worst that must be spoken.

speaker: SCROOP
text: Your uncle York is joined with Bolingbroke,

speaker: SCROOP
text: And all your northern castles yielded up,

speaker: SCROOP
text: And all your southern gentlemen in arms

speaker: SCROOP
text: Upon his party.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Thou hast said enough.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text:
To
                 
                Aumerle
                .
                 
              Beshrew thee, cousin, which didst lead me forth

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Of that sweet way I was in to despair.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What say you now? What comfort have we now?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: By heaven, I’ll hate him everlastingly

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That bids me be of comfort anymore.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Go to Flint Castle. There I’ll pine away;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: A king, woe’s slave, shall kingly woe obey.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That power I have, discharge, and let them go

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To ear the land that hath some hope to grow,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For I have none. Let no man speak again

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To alter this, for counsel is but vain.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: My liege, one word.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: He does me double wrong

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That wounds me with the flatteries of his tongue.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Discharge my followers. Let them hence away,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: From Richard’s night to Bolingbroke’s fair day.
